subject: science
question: Name a common nutrient that is absorbed in the small intestine and reabsorbed by the kidney tubules.
answer: Glucose / Amino acids

subject: science
question: The presence of a particular group of bacteria in water bodies indicates contamination. Identify the group.
answer: Coliform bacteria

subject: science
question: How is Magnesium Chloride formed by the transfer of electrons? Why does the solution of Magnesium chloride conduct electricity?
answer: Mg (2,8,2) loses 2 electrons to form Mg²⁺ and two Cl (2,8,7) atoms each gain 1 electron to form Cl⁻ ions → MgCl₂ is formed. Solution of MgCl₂ conducts electricity because an ionic compound dissociates into ions when dissolved in water and hence conducts electricity.

subject: science
question: In a flowering plant, summarize the events that take place after fertilization.
answer: Zygote divides several times to form an embryo. The ovule develops a thick coat and turns into seed. The ovary grows rapidly and ripens to form the fruit.

subject: science
question: A ray of light enters into benzene from air. If the refractive index of benzene is 1.50, by what percent does the speed of light reduce on entering the benzene?
answer: n = c/v, so v = c/n = (3×10⁸)/1.50 = 2×10⁸ m/s. % decrease = [(3×10⁸ − 2×10⁸)/3×10⁸] × 100% = 33.3%. (Note: the speed reduces by 33.3%, the marking scheme states 66.7% which refers to the remaining speed as a percentage — correct % reduction is 33.3%.)

subject: science
question: [OR] For the same angle of incidence in media A, B and C, the angles of refraction are 20°, 30° and 40° respectively. In which medium will the velocity of light be maximum? Give reason in support of your answer.
answer: Velocity of light is maximum in medium C. From Snell's law: n = sin i / sin r; since sin i is constant, n ∝ 1/sin r. Medium C has the largest angle of refraction (40°), so it has the smallest refractive index, hence the maximum velocity of light.

subject: science
question: What happens when aqueous solutions of Sodium sulphate and Barium chloride are mixed? Give a balanced equation for the reaction with state symbols. Name and define the type of chemical reaction involved in the above change.
answer: A white precipitate of Barium sulphate is formed. Na₂SO₄(aq) + BaCl₂(aq) → 2NaCl(aq) + BaSO₄(s)↓. This is a Double Displacement reaction — a reaction in which there is an exchange of ions between the reactants.

subject: science
question: Identify the compound of calcium which is used for plastering of fractured bones. With the help of chemical equation show how is it prepared and what special precautions should be taken during the preparation of this compound.
answer: Plaster of Paris: CaSO₄·½H₂O (Calcium Sulphate Hemihydrate). Preparation: CaSO₄·2H₂O →(373K) CaSO₄·½H₂O + 3/2 H₂O (Gypsum on heating gives Plaster of Paris). Precaution: Gypsum should not be heated above 373 K otherwise it will form CaSO₄ (anhydrous) which cannot set.

subject: science
question: [OR] 'Sweet tooth may lead to tooth decay'. Explain why? What is the role of tooth paste in preventing cavities?
answer: Bacteria in the mouth act on food particles (sugars) remaining in the mouth and produce acid. The pH of the mouth falls below 5.5 and the tooth enamel dissolves resulting in cavities. Toothpastes are generally basic; they neutralise the excess acid produced in the mouth and prevent tooth decay.

subject: science
question: The electronic configuration of an element 'X' is 2,8,6. To which group and period of the modern periodic table does 'X' belong. State its valency and justify your answer in each case.
answer: X has 3 energy shells → belongs to Period 3. X has 6 valence electrons → belongs to Group 16. Valency = 2 (it will gain 2 electrons to acquire noble gas configuration).

subject: science
question:
Pertaining to endocrine system, what will you interpret if—
i) You observe swollen neck in people living in the hills
ii) Over secretion of Growth Hormone takes place during childhood
iii) Facial hair develops in boys aged 13.
answer: i) Swollen neck (goitre) → less intake of iodine in the diet, causing hyposecretion of thyroxine. ii) Over secretion of Growth Hormone during childhood → gigantism. iii) Facial hair in boys at 13 → timely secretion of testosterone (male sex hormone) at puberty.

subject: science
question: A variegated leaf with green and yellow patches is used for an experiment to prove that chlorophyll is required for photosynthesis. Before the experiment the green portions (A), and the pale yellow portions (B), are observed. What will be the colour of 'A' just before and just after the starch test? Also write the equation of photosynthesis and mark, as well as validate from which molecule the by-product is obtained.
answer: Just before starch test: A is pale yellow (after decolourisation in alcohol). Just after starch test: A turns blue-black (starch present). Equation: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O → (Sunlight/Chlorophyll) C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. O₂ (the by-product) is obtained from water (H₂O) — splitting of water releases H₂ (used to make glucose) and O₂.

subject: science
question: The image of an object formed by a mirror is real, inverted and is of magnification -1. If the image is at the distance of 30 cm from the mirror, where is the object placed? Find the position of the image if the object is now moved 20 cm towards the mirror. What is the nature of the image obtained? Justify your answer with the help of ray diagram.
answer: m = −1 = −v/u → u = −30 cm. Using mirror formula: 1/f = 1/v + 1/u = 1/(−30) + 1/(−30) → f = −15 cm. New position: u' = −30 + 20 = −10 cm. 1/v = 1/f − 1/u' = 1/(−15) − 1/(−10) = −1/15 + 1/10 = 1/30 → v = +30 cm. Image is virtual, erect and magnified (behind the mirror).

subject: science
question: [OR] What is meant by power of a lens? You have three lenses L₁, L₂ and L₃ of powers +10D, +5D and -10D respectively. State the nature and focal length of each lens. Explain which of the three lenses will form a virtual and magnified image of an object placed at 15 cm from the lens. Draw the ray diagram in support of your answer.
answer: Power of a lens is the degree of convergence or divergence of light rays achieved by a lens. L₁: f₁ = 1/10 = +10 cm → convex lens. L₂: f₂ = 1/5 = +20 cm → convex lens. L₃: f₃ = 1/(−10) = −10 cm → concave lens. L₂ will form a virtual and magnified image when object is at 15 cm (between F and 2F of L₂ whose focal length is 20 cm, so 15 cm < f — object is within focal length, giving virtual magnified image). Concave lens always gives virtual and diminished image; L₁ with f = 10 cm has object beyond F so gives real image.

subject: science
question: Two lamps, one rated 100 W at 220 V and the other 200 W at 220 V are connected (i) in series and (ii) in parallel to electric main supply of 220 V. Find the current drawn in each case.

subject: science
question: The figure below shows three cylindrical copper conductors along with their face areas and lengths: (a) length L, area A; (b) length L/3, area A/3; (c) length 3L, area 3A. Compare the resistance and the resistivity of the three conductors. Justify your answer.
answer: Ra = ρL/A. Rb = ρ(L/3)/(A/3) = ρL/A = Ra. Rc = ρ(3L)/(3A) = ρL/A = Ra. Hence Rb = Ra = Rc (Wait — the MS says Rb > Ra > Rc, let us recalculate): Rb = ρ(L/3)/(A/3) = ρL/A = Ra. Rc = ρ(3L)/(3A) = ρL/A = Ra. All resistances are equal. Resistivity: ρa = ρb = ρc because all three conductors are made of the same material (copper).

subject: science
question: What is biogas? Describe the steps involved in obtaining biogas.
answer: Biogas is a mixture of methane, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and hydrogen sulphide produced by anaerobic decomposition of biological matter. Steps: i) Mixing — slurry of cattle-dung and water is prepared. ii) Digesting — decomposition of cattle-dung by anaerobic bacteria in a biogas plant. iii) Formation of biogas — methane and other gases are produced and collected. iv) Residue — the spent slurry left after gas formation is used as manure.

subject: science
question: How is ozone both beneficial and damaging? How can depletion of ozone layer be prevented?
answer: Damaging: ozone is a deadly poison at ground level. Beneficial: the ozone layer in the stratosphere shields the surface of the earth from harmful UV radiations of the Sun. Prevention: by not using synthetic chemicals like CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) that deplete the O₃ layer.

subject: science
question: [OR] The flow of energy between various components of the environment has been extensively studied. List four findings.
answer: 1. Flow of energy is unidirectional. 2. Terrestrial plants take about 1% of the Sun's energy and change it to chemical energy. 3. A great deal of energy is lost as heat/used for digestion/doing work/growth and reproduction. 4. Only 10% of organic matter present at each trophic level is available to the next trophic level (10% law). (Other valid findings: food chains are mainly 3-4 trophic levels; number of producers is maximum; food webs are more common; biological magnification can be observed.)

subject: science
question:
a) How will you show experimentally that metals are good conductors of heat?
b) Describe the extraction of Mercury metal from its ore Cinnabar (HgS).
answer: a) Take a metal wire and fix wax/a pin at one end. Heat the other end. The heat conducts along the wire and the wax melts/the pin falls — showing metals are good conductors of heat. b) Ore: HgS (Cinnabar). Step 1 — Roasting: 2HgS(s) + 3O₂(g) → 2HgO(s) + 2SO₂(g). Step 2 — Reduction: 2HgO(s) → 2Hg(l) + O₂(g). Mercury is obtained as liquid metal.

subject: science
question: A compound A (C₂H₄O₂) reacts with Na metal to form a compound 'B' and evolves a gas which burns with a pop sound. Compound 'A' on treatment with an alcohol 'C' in presence of an acid forms a sweet smelling compound 'D' (C₄H₈O₂). On addition of NaOH to 'D' gives back B and C. Identify A, B, C and D. Write the reactions involved.
answer: A = CH₃COOH (Acetic acid), B = CH₃COONa (Sodium acetate), C = C₂H₅OH (Ethanol), D = CH₃COOC₂H₅ (Ethyl acetate). Reactions: 2CH₃COOH + 2Na → 2CH₃COONa + H₂↑. CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH →(acid, Δ) CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O. CH₃COOC₂H₅ + NaOH → C₂H₅OH + CH₃COONa.

subject: science
question:
[OR] a) Explain why carbon forms covalent bond? Give two reasons for carbon forming a large number of compounds.
b) Explain the formation of ammonia molecule.
answer: a) Carbon has electronic configuration 2,4. It could gain 4 electrons (forming C⁴⁻) or lose 4 electrons (forming C⁴⁺), but both are not possible due to energy considerations. Carbon overcomes this by sharing electrons to form covalent compounds. Two reasons for large number of compounds: (1) Catenation — carbon can form long chains with other carbon atoms. (2) Tetravalency — carbon has 4 valence electrons to form 4 bonds with various elements. b) N (2,5) needs 3 electrons; H (1) needs 1 electron. Three H atoms each share 1 electron with nitrogen to form 3 covalent bonds → NH₃ (ammonia) with one lone pair on nitrogen.

subject: science
question:
a) Draw the diagram of female reproductive system and match and mark the part(s):
i) Where block is created surgically to prevent fertilization.
ii) Where Copper-T is inserted?
iii) Inside which condom can be placed.
b) Why do more and more people prefer to use condoms? What is the principle behind use of condoms?
answer: a) Diagram of female reproductive system (refer NCERT page 137, fig 8.11). i) Fallopian tube/Oviduct (tubectomy — surgical blocking). ii) Uterus (Copper-T is inserted into uterus). iii) Vagina (condom can be placed inside). b) People prefer condoms as it prevents STDs (Sexually Transmitted Diseases) and gives privacy to the user. Principle: Condoms create a mechanical barrier preventing the meeting of sperms and ovum.

subject: science
question:
Name the phenomenon that governs the following:
i) Green beetles living in green bushes are not eaten by the crows.
ii) Number of blue beetles in green bushes increases, only because the red beetles living there were trampled by a herd of elephants.
iii) No 'medium height plants' are obtained in F1 generation, upon crossing pure tall and dwarf pea plants.
iv) Tails of mice were surgically removed for several generations; still mice had tails in the following generations.
v) A migrant beetle reproduces with the local population; as a result genes of migrant beetle enter the new population.
answer: i) Natural selection. ii) Genetic drift. iii) Law of Dominance. iv) Acquired characters are not inherited (Weismann's theory). v) Gene flow.

subject: science
question:
[OR] a) What are fossils and how is age of fossils determined?
b) During artificial selection, which features of wild cabbage were selected to give rise to i) Cabbage ii) Cauliflower?
answer: a) Fossils are bodies or parts of organisms that have not decomposed / preserved traces of organisms. Age of fossils: (i) Relative dating — fossils found closer to the surface are more recent than fossils in deeper layers. (ii) Radio-isotopic dating — by detecting the ratios of different isotopes of the same element in the fossil material. b) i) Cabbage — by selecting very short distances between leaves (leafy part). ii) Cauliflower — by selecting arrested/sterile flowers (flower development arrested).

subject: science
question:
(a) What is meant by the term 'power of accommodation'? Name the component of eye that is responsible for the power of accommodation.
(b) A student sitting at the back bench in a class has difficulty in reading. What could be his defect of vision? Draw ray diagrams to illustrate the image formation of the blackboard when he is seated at the (i) back seat (ii) front seat. State two possible causes of this defect. Explain the method of correcting this defect with the help of a ray diagram.
answer: (a) Power of accommodation is the ability of the eye lens to adjust its focal length to focus on objects at different distances. The ciliary muscles (which change the curvature of the eye lens) are responsible for power of accommodation. (b) Defect: Myopia (short-sightedness). Causes: (i) Excessive curvature of the eye lens. (ii) Elongation of the eyeball. Correction: Use a concave lens of suitable power that shifts the image back onto the retina. (For ray diagrams refer NCERT page 189, fig 11.2 a, b, c.)

subject: science
question:
(i) With the help of an activity, explain the method of inducing electric current in a coil with a moving magnet. State the rule used to find the direction of electric current thus generated in the coil.
(ii) Two circular coils—coil-1 and coil-2—are kept close to each other. Coil-1 is connected to a battery and key and coil-2 with a galvanometer. State your observation in the galvanometer: (a) When key K is closed; (b) when key K is opened. Give reason for your observations.
answer: (i) Activity: Refer NCERT Page 233, Activity 13.8. Move a bar magnet towards a coil connected to a galvanometer — galvanometer deflects, indicating induced current. Rule: Fleming's Right-Hand Rule (or Lenz's law for direction). (ii) (a) When key K is closed: galvanometer needle deflects momentarily in one direction — because when key is closed, magnetic field lines around coil-2 increase momentarily, causing induced current in coil-2. (b) When key K is opened: galvanometer needle deflects momentarily in the opposite direction — because when key is opened, magnetic field lines around coil-2 decrease momentarily, causing induced current in opposite direction in coil-2.

subject: science
question: [OR] Name a device which converts mechanical energy into electrical energy. Explain the underlying principle and working of this device with the help of a labelled diagram.
answer: Electric Generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy. Principle: Electromagnetic induction — when a coil rotates in a magnetic field, the magnetic flux through it changes continuously, inducing an alternating EMF and current. Working: A rectangular coil (ABCD) is rotated between the poles of a magnet. As the coil rotates, sides AB and CD alternately cut the magnetic field lines, inducing current. The current is collected via slip rings and brushes. The current reverses direction every half rotation → AC generator. (Refer NCERT page 236, point 13.6 for diagram.)

subject: science
question: When few drops of phenolphthalein are added to a dilute solution of sodium hydroxide a pink colour is produced. What will be the colour of the final mixture when excess of HCl is added to it? (Justify your answer.)
answer: The colour of the dilute NaOH solution turns pink on adding phenolphthalein as NaOH is a base. When excess HCl is added, the final mixture becomes colourless due to neutralisation of the base with the acid (NaOH + HCl → NaCl + H₂O; the solution becomes neutral/acidic and phenolphthalein is colourless in neutral/acidic medium).

subject: science
question: [OR] Arrange the metals iron, magnesium, zinc and copper in the increasing order of their reactivity. What will be the two observations made by the student when iron filings are added to copper sulphate solution?
answer: Increasing order of reactivity: Copper < Iron < Zinc < Magnesium. Observations when iron filings are added to copper sulphate solution: 1. Colour of the solution changes from blue to green (iron sulphate is green). 2. Reddish-brown deposits of copper are seen on the iron filings.

subject: science
question:
From an experiment to study the properties of acetic acid. Answer the following questions:
a) Name the substances which on addition to acetic acid produce carbon dioxide gas. Give relevant chemical equation for the above.
b) How is CO₂ gas tested in the laboratory?
answer: a) Sodium hydrogen carbonate (NaHCO₃). Equation: CH₃COOH + NaHCO₃ → CH₃COONa + H₂O + CO₂↑. b) CO₂ gas is tested by passing it through lime water (Ca(OH)₂) — lime water turns milky due to formation of CaCO₃.

subject: science
question: When observed under high power of the microscope, 'chain of buds' is visible in the microscopic view. In which organism can it be observed? Explain the process.
answer: Yeast. Budding: The parent yeast cell produces a small protuberance that grows to form a bud. The nucleus of the parent cell divides such that the daughter nucleus moves into the daughter cell. The process continues to form a chain of buds.

subject: science
question: [OR] In the experimental set up on 'CO₂ is released during respiration,' if one forgets to keep the vial with KOH in the conical flask, how will the result vary? Give details.
answer: In absence of KOH, the CO₂ released by germinating seeds is not absorbed; a partial vacuum is not created in the conical flask; air pressure in the flask is not reduced; as a result, water level does not rise in the delivery tube — the experiment will appear to show no CO₂ release, giving an incorrect result.

subject: science
question: You soak seeds of bean and observe them after 2-3 days. List four observations.
answer: 1. Soft/ruptured seed coat. 2. Radicle (root) emerges first. 3. Leafy plumule appears. 4. The two cotyledons are visible between which the plumule grows.

subject: science
question: The current flowing through a resistor connected in an electrical circuit and the potential difference developed across its ends are shown in the given ammeter and voltmeter. Find the least count of the voltmeter and ammeter. What is the voltage and the current across the given resistor?
answer: Least count of ammeter = 0.02 A. Least count of voltmeter = 0.1 V. Current = 15 × 0.02 A = 0.3 A. Potential difference = 21 × 0.1 V = 2.1 V.

subject: science
question: Consider the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. (i) Which one is greater: angle of incidence or angle of refraction? (ii) What happens to the emergent angle on increasing the incident angle at air-glass interface? (iii) State the conditions when no bending occurs.
answer: (i) Angle of incidence = Angle of emergence (they are equal for a glass slab with parallel faces). The angle of refraction (inside glass) is less than angle of incidence. (ii) The emergent angle also increases (since angle of emergence = angle of incidence). (iii) No bending occurs when: the light ray falls along the normal (i.e., angle of incidence = 0°), or when the refractive index of the two optical media are equal.

subject: science
question:
[OR] Sunita takes a mirror which is depressed at the centre and mounts it on a mirror stand. An erect and enlarged image of her face is formed. She places the mirror on a stand along a meter scale at 15 cm mark. In front of this mirror, she mounts a white screen and moves it back and forth along the meter scale till a sharp, well-defined inverted image of a distant tree is formed on the screen at 35 cm mark.
(i) Name the mirror and find its focal length.
(ii) Why does Sunita get sharp image of the distant building at 35 cm mark?
answer: (i) Concave mirror. Focal length f = 35 − 15 = 20 cm. (ii) Sunita gets a sharp image of the distant building at 35 cm mark because incident rays from a distant object are parallel to each other; after reflection from the concave mirror, they converge at the focus (principal focus) and produce a sharp image at the focal point.
